notes: All 8 steps completed in one iteration. All 43 tests pass (28 memory_service + 15 durable_queue). Four bugs fixed: (1) `and` -> `or` in success flag, (2) mem0 error surfaced in response message, (3) close() now closes all 5 sub-resources with suppress(Exception), (4) enqueue_batch has try/except rollback. Tests confirmed failing before each fix and passing after.

summary: Write failing test: add_memory success=False when single targeted store (Mem0-only) fails. In TestAddMemory, add test_success_false_when_only_targeted_store_fails: set category='preferences_and_norms' (Mem0-only, not dual_write), mock mem0.add to raise, add mock _write_journal, call add_memory, assert _write_journal.log_write_op was called with success=False. Currently passes because `not (None and error)` = `not None` = True.; Fix Bug 1: In memory_service.py line 447, change `success=not (_graphiti_error and _mem0_error)` to `success=not (_graphiti_error or _mem0_error)`. This ensures success=False when ANY store errors, not just when ALL stores error.; Write failing test: mem0 error appears in response message. In TestAddMemory, add test_mem0_error_surfaced_in_response: set category='preferences_and_norms', mock mem0.add to raise ValueError('qdrant connection refused'), call add_memory, assert 'mem0_error' substring appears in result.message. Currently fails because only _graphiti_error is appended to msg.; Fix Bug 2: After the existing `if _graphiti_error:` block (line 466-467), add parallel `if _mem0_error: msg += f' [mem0_error: {_mem0_error}]'` to surface Mem0 errors in the response message.; Write failing test: close() calls all sub-resource close methods. Add new TestClose class in test_memory_service.py. Set up service with mock _write_journal (AsyncMock with close), mock _event_buffer (AsyncMock with close), and mock mem0 (AsyncMock with close). Call service.close(). Assert all five close methods called: durable_queue.close(), graphiti.close(), mem0.close(), _write_journal.close(), _event_buffer.close(). Currently fails because close() only calls first two.; Fix Bug 3: (a) Add async def close() to Mem0Backend in mem0_client.py — iterate _instances, close each instance's vector_store.client if it has a close method, then clear _instances. (b) Update MemoryService.close() to also call self.mem0.close(), self._write_journal.close() (if set), and self._event_buffer.close() (if set). Use contextlib.suppress(Exception) around each to ensure all resources attempt cleanup.; Write failing test: enqueue_batch rolls back on mid-batch exception. Add TestEnqueueBatchRollback class in test_durable_queue.py. Enqueue a batch of 3 items where the 2nd item is missing 'operation' key (triggers KeyError). Assert the exception is raised, then check DB has 0 pending items (verifying rollback). Currently fails because no rollback leaves partial inserts.; Fix Bug 4: In durable_queue.py enqueue_batch(), replace `async with self._db.execute('BEGIN'):` with explicit transaction management: `await self._db.execute('BEGIN')` then wrap the for loop + commit in try/except, calling `await self._db.rollback()` on exception before re-raising.

Step 9: Added TestMem0BackendClose class that directly tests Mem0Backend.close() await path. Injects AsyncMock client into _instances, calls await backend.close(), asserts client.close was awaited once and _instances cleared. Test confirmed failing (0 awaits vs 1 expected) + RuntimeWarning about unawaited coroutine. Step 10: Fixed by adding `await` to `client.close()` in mem0_client.py line 197. All 44 tests pass.

All 6 plan steps completed. Step 1: TestDropVectorIndices (3 failing tests) for GraphitiBackend.drop_vector_indices(). Step 2: Implemented drop_vector_indices() in graphiti_client.py — calls list_indices(), filters type=='VECTOR', calls drop_index() for each, returns [{label,field}] list. Step 3: Added 6 new tests to TestReindexAndReplay for drop_indices parameter (order tracking, skip when False, default False, indices_dropped in result). Step 4: Enhanced reindex_and_replay() with drop_indices=False param — calls backend.drop_vector_indices() before reindex() when True, includes indices_dropped in return dict. Step 5: Added 2 failing tests for run_reindex(drop_indices=...). Step 6: Updated run_reindex() with drop_indices=False param, passes to reindex_and_replay(), added --drop-indices and --config argparse flags to __main__ block. 51 total tests pass.
